description_long: This is an upright, arched gravestone made of weathered stone, partially embedded in the ground and surrounded by scattered earth and debris. The front face features a carved relief of a lamb at the top, followed by an inscription panel with the name 'Mr. Jenkins' and additional epitaph text, though some of the writing is worn and difficult to read. The stone has a rounded top and straight sides, with visible signs of aging such as discoloration, moss, and surface erosion. The back of the gravestone is plain and unadorned, showing a uniform, aged stone texture. The sides are narrow and reveal the thickness of the stone, with some dirt and organic material at the base. The overall appearance suggests a traditional 19th-century grave marker, likely found in a cemetery or graveyard setting.
description: A weathered, upright stone gravestone with a rounded top, carved lamb relief, and worn inscription, partially embedded in earth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the carved lamb relief, inscription panel, and weathered stone surface with dirt at the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the gravestone and the accumulation of earth around its base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned back of the gravestone with visible aging and discoloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stone's thickness and more earth and debris at the base.

license_info.creator_display_name: Santa Cruz Museum of Art and History
